Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for emails to omar.ibrahim@moveops.com using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "expense" or "reimbursement") to find three complaint emails: Maria Santos (Vectral, $550 standing desk rejected), Lisa Park (Vectral, $130 WiFi rejected), Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89 keyboard pending for weeks).
- Read each complaint email using get_email_by_id to get full details and capture the from_addr needed for replies later.
- Search for Julian Brooks's message in #customer-support (C007) using channels_list and conversations_history or conversations_search_messages. Find his warning about the three complaints, Vectral sensitivity, finance flagging suspicious stipend activity, and his directive to get the full picture and notify the AMs.
- Search Airtable stipend transactions for each complainant using airtable_search_records (queries: "Maria Santos", "Lisa Park", "Andre Washington"). Find: Maria Santos and Lisa Park both auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale default limits (pending manual correction); Andre Washington flagged with "Duplicate Hash" audit flag - receipt identical to Kevin Tran's submission.
- Search Airtable broadly for all flagged or rejected stipend records using airtable_search_records (queries: "Vectral", "Mosaic", "rejected", "flagged" or similar). Discover the full scope: 5 Vectral false rejections from stale config (2 already corrected by Mina, 3 still pending), 3 Mosaic false approvals from a separate malformed JSON config bug ($1,685 total), and 3 fraud-flagged TechComfort Supply duplicate receipts across Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop.
- Search Airtable client accounts (tblClientAccts01) for Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop using airtable_search_records to identify account managers - Vectral: Mina Hashimoto, Mosaic: Emeka Diallo, BrightLoop: Mina Hashimoto.
- Search Slack across #engineering (C003) and #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_search_messages (queries: "ExpenseBot", "expense", "Vectral expense" or similar), then read threads using conversations_replies. Discover prior discussions about the false rejections, false approvals, Dmitri's full audit of 47 expenses ($885 exposure) relayed by Samira to Elena, and Elena's directive pausing auto-decisions.
- Search Slack and email for the duplicate receipt fraud investigation using conversations_search_messages (queries: "TechComfort", "duplicate", "receipt hash" or similar) in #engineering and #finance (C005), and search_emails for related threads. Discover: Dmitri found identical SHA-256 hashes between Kevin Tran ($89) and Andre Washington ($89), plus Jordan Ekwueme ($45) with matching PDF template metadata. Marcus placed admin holds on Andre and Jordan; Kevin's $89 already paid on 3/8.
- Search Linear for ExpenseBot issues using linear_list_issues (project: proj_expense_autocategorizer) and linear_list_comments. Find: ENG-214 and ENG-215 (P1 config fixes) in_progress, ENG-216 (full audit) in_progress, ENG-220 (duplicate detection) todo. Fixes underway but not complete.
- Search CRM using crm_search_deals (query: "Vectral") to find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at dealstage "at_risk" - Rachel Whitfield demanding 15% discount citing ExpenseBot false rejection, fraud audit friction, and onboarding timeline issues.
- Search emails using search_emails and get_email_by_id (queries: "expense audit", "Vectral", "TechComfort", "discount" or similar) to find internal communication chain: Mina's escalation, Emeka's false approval report, Dmitri's audit findings, Marcus's hold confirmations, Samira's audit summary, and Rachel Whitfield's discount demand.
- Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "stipend" or "STIP" or similar) to find payment status: STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran, $89, Balance = 0, already paid), STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, $89, admin hold), STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, $45, admin hold). Confirms $89 out the door and $134 on hold.
- Look up Julian Brooks's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Julian") to find julian.brooks@moveops.com.
- Look up Mina Hashimoto's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina") to find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
- Look up Emeka Diallo's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka") to find emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
- Send email (via send_email) to julian.brooks@moveops.com with full investigation findings covering: the 3 complaints and root causes, Vectral false rejections (5 employees, 3 pending), Mosaic false approvals ($1,685), TechComfort fraud across 3 clients, payment status, Vectral account risk, engineering fix status, and which cases need action vs. which holds should stay.
- Send email (via send_email) to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with heads-up: 3 pending Vectral corrections (Maria Santos $550, Lisa Park $130, James Okafor $82) and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) fraud-flagged $45 on hold.
- Send email (via send_email) to emeka.diallo@moveops.com with heads-up: Andre Washington's $89 on admin hold (duplicate receipt), plus the false approval exposure ($1,200 + $350 + $135) that MoveOps is absorbing.
- Send reply (via send_email) to maria.santos@vectralsystems.com acknowledging her complaint and confirming it was a system error being corrected.
- Send reply (via send_email) to lisa.park@vectralsystems.com acknowledging her complaint and confirming it was a system error being corrected.
- Send reply (via send_email) to andre.washington@mosaicrobotics.com acknowledging the delay and letting him know the team is looking into it - does not reveal fraud investigation details.
- Post a shift rundown in #customer-support (C007) using conversations_add_message summarizing findings, actions taken (Julian briefed, AMs notified, complainants acknowledged), and status of each situation.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 ```json The model must use the get_email_by_id tool. ```
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The trajectory contains no call to get_email_by_id. The agent only used search_emails with various queries but never fetched full email content via get_email_by_id.
✗Run #2The trajectory contains no call to get_email_by_id. The agent used search_emails multiple times but never fetched any individual email by ID to retrieve full content including sender addresses.
✗Run #3The agent never called get_email_by_id. It only used search_emails with various queries (e.g., 'stipend', 'Vectral', 'Mosaic', 'Kevin Tran', 'Andre Washington', etc.) but never retrieved full email contents by ID.
✗Run #4No call to get_email_by_id appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent only used search_emails to find emails but never fetched individual email details using get_email_by_id.
✗Run #5No call to get_email_by_id appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent used search_emails and list_emails but never retrieved individual email contents via get_email_by_id.
✗Run #6The agent never called get_email_by_id. It used list_emails and search_emails but did not read individual emails by ID to capture sender addresses and full content.
2 ```json The model must use the airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records tool. ```
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The trajectory contains both airtable_search_records (field_name: 'Approval Status', value: 'Rejected') and airtable_list_records calls against the 'Stipend Transactions' table.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (searching Stipend Transactions by 'Rejected' and 'Pending' status, searching Client Accounts for 'Vectral', 'Mosaic', 'BrightLoop'), and also called airtable_list_tables and airtable_list_bases.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times — searching by Approval Status (Rejected, Flagged, On Hold), by Client (Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop), and by Employee Name (Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme, Kevin Tran).
✓Run #5The trajectory contains multiple calls to airtable_search_records (for Rejected status, Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme, Danielle Osei, Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop client accounts).
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_records twice (for 'Stipend Transactions' and 'Client Accounts' tables), satisfying this criterion.

tool selection

tool selection

5 ```json The model must use the crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies tool. ```
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The trajectory contains no call to crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies. Despite the agent's output referencing Rachel Whitfield's 15% discount demand and the Vectral renegotiation, there is no CRM tool call in the trajectory to source this data.
✗Run #2The trajectory contains no calls to crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies. The agent never queried the CRM for Vectral account or deal information. Despite correctly reporting the contract renegotiation details in its output (suggesting it somehow learned of them), no CRM tool call appears in the trajectory.
✗Run #3The agent never called crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies. It found Vectral account details via airtable_search_records on the Client Accounts table, which happened to contain negotiation notes, but the CRM-specific tools were not used.
✗Run #4No call to crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies appears in the trajectory. The agent found Vectral account information from Airtable's Client Accounts table instead.
✗Run #5No call to crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies appears in the trajectory. The agent obtained Vectral renegotiation context from Slack (#finance channel history) rather than the CRM.
✗Run #6The agent never called crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies. Despite Julian's warning about Vectral's sensitivity, no CRM lookup was performed. The renewal deal information appears in the agent's response but was not sourced from the CRM.
6 ```json The model must use the quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill tool. ```
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill. It used quickbooks_search_invoices instead, which is a different tool. The payment status information (STIP-2026-0089, STIP-2026-0094, etc.) in the response cannot be verified as coming from the correct tool.
✗Run #2The trajectory contains no call to quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill. The agent only called quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria {DocNumber: 'INV-2026'}, which is not the correct tool or query for finding stipend payment records (STIP-2026 series bills).
✗Run #3The agent never called quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill. Payment status information (Kevin Tran already paid, others on hold) was derived from Airtable records and Slack messages, not from QuickBooks.
✗Run #4No call to quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill appears in the trajectory. The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (twice) but never the bills-specific tools needed to find STIP-2026 series payment records.
✗Run #5No call to quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill appears in the trajectory. The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (with criteria DocNumber: INV-2026) but never used the bills-specific tools. Payment status data was obtained from Slack messages.
✗Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (not quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill). The stipend payment records (STIP-2026 series) are bills, not invoices. The agent used the wrong QuickBooks tool.
7 ```text The model must pass a query related to 'Vectral' (or similar) when searching Airtable stipend records to discover the full scope of false rejections beyond the two complainants. ```
query construction
 ✓✗✗✗✓✗ 33%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_search_records with field_name='Account' and value='Vectral', satisfying the requirement to search Airtable with a Vectral-related query.
✗Run #2Neither Airtable call used a Vectral-specific query or filter. The airtable_search_records call filtered on field 'Approval Status' = 'Rejected', and airtable_list_records had no filter at all. No Vectral-scoped query was passed.
✗Run #3The agent searched Airtable Stipend Transactions using field_name 'Approval Status' with values 'Rejected' and 'Pending' — not a Vectral-specific query. The airtable_search_records call with 'Vectral' was on the Client Accounts table, not the Stipend Transactions table.
✗Run #4The agent searched airtable_search_records for Stipend Transactions by Approval Status values (Rejected, Flagged, On Hold) and by individual employee names, but never used 'Vectral' as a query/filter on the Stipend Transactions table to find the full scope of Vectral false rejections.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records with table_name 'Client Accounts' and value 'Vectral', and also searched Stipend Transactions records for individual Vectral employees. The Vectral client account search directly targets all Vectral-scoped data.
✗Run #6The agent called airtable_list_records with no filter or query parameter related to 'Vectral'. It retrieved all records from the Stipend Transactions table without filtering by client, meaning the criterion requiring a Vectral-scoped query is not met. However, it did retrieve all records and found the Vectral data within them.
8 ```text The model must pass a query related to 'stipend', 'STIP', or a relevant employee name (e.g., 'Kevin Tran', 'Andre Washington', or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_bills to find payment records. ```
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called quickbooks_search_bills. It called quickbooks_search_invoices with DocNumber='stipend', but since the required tool was never used, this criterion is not met.
✗Run #2The only QuickBooks call was quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria {DocNumber: 'INV-2026'}, which is neither the correct tool (bills vs invoices) nor a stipend/STIP-related query. No quickbooks_search_bills call was made with any stipend, STIP, or employee name query.
✗Run #3The agent never called quickbooks_search_bills at all, so no query with 'stipend', 'STIP', or employee names was passed to QuickBooks.
✗Run #4No call to quickbooks_search_bills was made at all in the trajectory. The agent used quickbooks_search_invoices instead, which would not find the STIP-2026-XXXX bill records.
✗Run #5No call to quickbooks_search_bills was made in the trajectory at all. The only QuickBooks call was quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria DocNumber: INV-2026, which is neither stipend/STIP-related nor employee name-related, and targets invoices not bills.
✗Run #6The agent used quickbooks_search_invoices (wrong tool) with a null/empty criteria parameter. No query related to 'stipend', 'STIP', or relevant employee names was passed to any QuickBooks bills search.

query construction
